description: You are a franchise owner of a retail shipping logistics company. You have leased an empty commercial storefront and must draft the floor plan for your new neighborhood shipping and postal center to verify it can accommodate your operational needs. The facility must include a strict separation between public and employee-only zones. Build at least two interior partition walls to divide the space into a public Retail & Queue Area, a secure Back-of-House Sorting & Storage Area, and an alcove for PO Boxes. Place at least two access doors to control flow between zones. Furnish the Service Counter with at least three desks/counters, three computers/registers, and three stools/chairs. Furnish a Self-Service Packing area in the public zone with at least two high tables and two stools, and a Retail Display area with at least three shelving units. The Back-of-House Sorting area requires at least four large sorting tables and eight large shelving units for package storage. Place at least 45 total furniture items across all zones. Define the floor zones by creating rooms with distinct floor colors or textures for at least two rooms to differentiate the public retail area from the secure back-of-house area. Document clearances by placing at least three dimension lines in the public area to verify ADA queue width. Save the file in its current location.
